title: gagea glacialis var. joannis
intro: Gagea glacialis var. joannis, also known as the Alpine Adder's Tongue, is a species of flowering plant in the lily family. It is a perennial plant native to mountainous regions in Europe. The medicinal properties of Gagea glacialis var. joannis lie in its rhizomes, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant has been used to prepare herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
